## Missing briefing files silently degrade cabinet quality — but the files DO exist

**Friction:** Briefing files were generated by /onboard and exist in the project root (`_briefing.md`, `_briefing-identity.md`, `_briefing-architecture.md`, `_briefing-jurisdictions.md`, `_briefing-cabinet.md`, `_briefing-work-tracking.md`). But cabinet member SKILL.md files reference them as bare names (`_briefing.md`) and the audit/orient orchestration looks in `.claude/cabinet/` — never finding them. The result: every session, Claude says "no briefing file exists" and proceeds without project context. The user has corrected this in past sessions, at which point Claude immediately finds the files at the project root. But the fix never sticks — next session, same problem.

Three compounding issues: (1) /onboard generates briefing files to the project root, but cabinet members don't look there. Either onboard should put them where cabinet members expect, or cabinet members should search more broadly (root, `.claude/cabinet/`, `.claude/briefing/`). (2) When briefing files aren't found, nothing warns the user — audit and orient silently degrade. Should warn: "⚠ No cabinet briefing files found — run /onboard to generate them." (3) Individual cabinet member agents should note in their output meta when running without their expected briefing, not silently skip.

**Suggestion:** Fix the path mismatch — either update onboard to write briefings to `.claude/cabinet/` (where the output-contract.md references them), or update cabinet member resolution to search project root as a fallback. Add orient health check for missing briefings. Add audit pre-flight warning.

**Session context:** Running a focused UI audit (usability + mantine-quality) on Maginnis Howard mass arbitration admin. Both agents ran without briefing context despite /onboard having been run and 6 briefing files existing at project root.
